scope.context: Preflight status was ready. The scan reviewed 12 runtime/source rows from the working-tree snapshot, including the untracked approval view-model source file added as a supporting diff row.
limitations: Test-only files and the planning document were excluded from runtime security discovery but are listed in coverage exclusions.
scope.runtimeStatus: pnpm check, pnpm test, pnpm build, and git diff --check passed before security scan continuation.
scope.summary: Diff scan of Morrow beta.26 consumer-flow hardening changes touching CLI onboarding, project identity, approval rendering, terminal state, shared task-event contracts, and orchestrator tool recovery/progress behavior.
scope.validationMode: Discovery-only diff scan; validation and attack-path phases were skipped because no plausible candidates survived discovery.

assets: approval decisions and command trust patterns
task evidence and execution audit history
assumptions: The scan is diff-scoped to the selected working-tree snapshot
Morrow remains local-first beta software and not a hosted multi-tenant control plane
No reportable finding is emitted without candidate ledger receipts; no candidates survived discovery in this scan
attackerCapabilities: malicious prompt, repository content, file name, terminal output, browser page, model tool argument, generated patch, imported Hermes data, or local web request
operator misconfiguration or overbroad trust pattern
securityObjectives: filesystem and terminal tools stay inside explicit project/workspace boundaries
secrets are never exposed in prompts/logs/reports
task status and approval UI remain crash-resistant and honest
threatModel.summary: Morrow is a local-first personal AI agent whose sensitive boundaries are local project files, provider credentials, memory, approvals, tool execution, model context, schedules, plugins, browser/process control, and release artifacts.
trustBoundaries: untrusted model output to local tool runtime
registered project roots and worktrees to host filesystem
local context manager to external model providers
plugin/skill/MCP capability declarations to execution authority
